title: oxalis acetosella proles parviflora
intro: Oxalis acetosella proles parviflora, also known as the small-leaved wood-sorrel or small wood-sorrel, is a subspecies of the wood sorrel plant, belonging to the Oxalidaceae family. It is a perennial herb with small, round leaves and white or pinkish flowers that grow in woodland environments. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, particularly its ability to reduce acidity and act as a diuretic, making it useful for treating conditions such as urinary tract infections and kidney stones. Herbal preparations of Oxalis acetosella proles parviflora include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to promote digestive health and alleviate symptoms of arthritis and gout.
